seoTitle: How to Import Trello Cards From a CSV File
metaDescription: Turn a CSV spreadsheet into Trello cards using an import Power-Up, map columns like due date and assignee, and verify the results.
intro: Manually recreating a task list from a CSV file line by line is a waste of time when Trello has import tools built for exactly this. This guide covers structuring your CSV, connecting an import Power-Up, and mapping your columns to the right card fields.

steps.heading: Structure your CSV headers
steps.body: Set up baseline columns like card title, description, and target list before exporting your spreadsheet as a CSV.

steps.heading: Open the Power-Up folder
steps.body: On your destination board, click Power-Ups, then Add Power-Ups to browse available import tools.

steps.heading: Add and authorize an import tool
steps.body: Search for a tool like 'Import to Trello by Blue Cat' or 'Excelefy', click Add, then Authorize to complete the connection.

steps.heading: Upload your CSV
steps.body: Open the newly added import tool from your board menu and drag your saved CSV file into the upload box.

steps.heading: Map your fields
steps.body: Match your CSV headers to Trello fields like card name, description, and due date on the mapping screen.

steps.heading: Run the import
steps.body: Select the destination list for your new cards and click 'Check and Import' to trigger the batch process.

faq.question: What columns should my CSV file include?
faq.answer: At minimum, include a card title column, along with optional columns for description, due date, and target list.

faq.question: Can I import checklists and labels from a CSV?
faq.answer: Yes, dedicated import Power-Ups can map more complex fields like checklists, assignees, and labels beyond just the basics.

faq.question: What if my dates don't import correctly?
faq.answer: Date-time syntax mismatches are a common issue, so double-check your CSV date format matches what the import tool expects.

faq.question: Should I test with a small file first?
faq.answer: Yes, running a small test import helps you confirm your column mapping works before importing a large file.
